asset_name: Elemental Projectile Sheet — eight elements plus reflected and split states
shared_properties: Every projectile keeps a centered readable core, a motion-tail direction cue, and a glow edge for fast recognition.
Element colors follow the GDD matrix so card conversion results can be read in one glance.
Projectile silhouettes stay within a 96x96 frame and support both slow and fast travel speeds.
differences: This task covers hostile and reflected spell bodies only.
Timing windows, lane guides, and telegraph icons remain in separate tasks.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Moodboard for balancing warm hall lighting against saturated elemental glows.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette board for keeping projectile scale threatening without exceeding the target lane width.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/reflect_academy/trial_2/obstacles_elemental_projectile_sheet.png during DuelLoading and register the atlas under obstacles_elemental_projectile_sheet.
runtime_notes: Map frames to fire, water, thunder, wind, ice, poison, light, dark, reflected_return, and split_echo states in that order.
Keep the projectile pivot centered so Bevy rotation and sinusoidal movement do not offset the glow tail.
Allow a separate additive duplicate sprite for the light and thunder states when high-impact reflections occur.

context.usage: Spell bodies used for enemy attacks, converted returns, and split-reflection combo moments.

motion_notes: Fire uses a tapered teardrop core with 10px ember tail.
Water uses a rounded droplet with a 6px trailing splash arc.
Thunder and light states keep the smallest hit silhouette but the brightest glow halo.
Reflected return adds a cyan outer ring to show ownership change.

shape.silhouette: Compact orb and shard projectile sheet with distinct elemental shapes and glow tails.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 480x192px with ten 96x96 states and no padding.
Each of the eight elemental states reads as a different silhouette even before color is considered.
Reflected return and split echo states remain distinguishable from hostile projectiles during high-speed play.
Glow edges stay inside the 96x96 frame and preserve transparent margins for runtime batching.
